# UC-17.1.26 · Cloudflare Zone HTTP 5xx and Origin Error Rate

## Description

Elevated 5xx responses at the edge or from the origin indicate failing pools, overloaded applications, or deliberate application-layer attacks. Ray ID–level aggregation supports fast hand-off between CDN and application owners.

## Value

Protects revenue and SLA-backed APIs by detecting origin brownouts minutes before synthetic monitoring catches them on low-traffic paths.

## Implementation

Create a Logpush job for `http_requests` to Splunk HEC; set sourcetype to `cloudflare:json` if following Cloudflare’s Splunk integration guide, or the dataset-specific sourcetype from the app’s Details section. Include `EdgeResponseStatus`, `OriginResponseStatus`, `ClientRequestHost`, and `RayID` in field selection. Alert when 5xx count exceeds a rolling baseline per hostname.

## SPL

```spl
index=web sourcetype="cloudflare:json" earliest=-1h
| eval edge_status=tonumber(EdgeResponseStatus)
| eval origin_status=tonumber(OriginResponseStatus)
| where edge_status>=500 OR origin_status>=500
| stats count by ClientRequestHost, EdgeResponseStatus, OriginResponseStatus, RayID
| sort -count
| head 50
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Web.Web
  by Web.user, Web.url, Web.http_method span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart (5xx rate), Table (hostname, edge vs origin status), Single value (SLA breach indicator).

## References

- [Cloudflare Docs — Enable Logpush to Splunk](https://developers.cloudflare.com/logs/logpush/logpush-job/enable-destinations/splunk/)
- [Splunkbase — Cloudflare App for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4501)
